summary: Matched and linked the Dosei item translation unit by converting configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c and adjusting C source shape to satisfy the linker/matcher. The source changes mostly replace ad-hoc inline rotation setters and PAD_STACK padding with better-shaped helper inlines, explicit HSD_JObj rotation calls, sdata2 ordering control, user_data access in several functions, and small function-order/code-shape tweaks.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itdosei.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).

Added a static sdata2_ordering helper containing volatile float/double constants including 1.0f, 0.5f, 0.0f, M_PI_2, M_PI / 4.0f, M_PI, 4503601774854144.0, and 60.0f, apparently to force constant ordering needed for matching.
Removed placeholder/platform and some unused includes while adding it/itgroundcoll.h; the file now relies on existing item and baselib headers rather than placeholder scaffolding.

Replaced custom inline rotation setters itDosei_SetRotX/Y/Z and itDosei_SetFacingAngle/SetFacingAngleFC with a smaller itDosei_FacingAngle helper returning the computed angle, plus direct calls to HSD_JObjSetRotationY or HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty.
Introduced HSD_JObjSetRotationZeroWithMtxDirty to call HSD_JObjSetRotationXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, and HSD_JObjSetRotationZWithMtxDirty on gobj->hsd_obj.
Removed multiple PAD_STACK calls once matching was achieved through better source shaping.
Changed several Item pointer fetches from GET_ITEM(gobj) to direct gobj->user_data in picked-up, dropped, motion 9 anim, and damage-received paths.

Added small helper inlines around itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim, including itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim_inline, itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAnimSpeed, and itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAttrs, and included a noted 'permuterslop' null-check shape.
Moved itDosei_UnkMotion10_Phys and itDosei_UnkMotion10_Coll to after itDosei_UnkMotion10_Anim without changing their visible behavior.
PR body recorded project progress after the merge: Remaining item TUs: 18; Remaining item functions: 63.
smart_moves: Converted inline setters that duplicated HSD_JObj assert/dirty logic into calls to the canonical HSD_JObjSetRotation* and HSD_JObjSetRotation*WithMtxDirty APIs, reducing handwritten baselib internals while preserving matched code shape.
Separated angle calculation into itDosei_FacingAngle, allowing call sites to choose whether to use normal rotation setters or mtx-dirty rotation setters while sharing the same facing_dir math.
Used volatile constants in sdata2_ordering to influence .sdata2 ordering, a common tactic when source code is close but constant pool layout prevents linking/matching.
Eliminated PAD_STACK padding by finding more natural source-level changes, such as helper inlines, direct gobj->user_data access, and function ordering.
Kept behavior-oriented calls explicit at call sites, for example HSD_JObjSetRotationY(gobj->hsd_obj, itDosei_FacingAngle(...)), which documents that the helper only computes the angle.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, a successful match may require both C expression shaping and build configuration changes; remember to flip configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only after the object links.
When matching JObj rotation code, prefer existing HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z and WithMtxDirty APIs over manually reproducing assert and dirty-flag logic unless the original shape demands otherwise.

If a constant pool ordering mismatch blocks a match, a dedicated volatile helper can force float/double constants into the required order, but this should be treated as a matching tactic rather than gameplay logic.
Direct gobj->user_data access can sometimes match where GET_ITEM(gobj) macro expansion does not; use evidence from generated code before choosing between them.
Small inline helper extraction can be enough to remove artificial PAD_STACK usage and align register/stack behavior.
Function order within a C file can matter for matching/linking in this project, as shown by moving Motion10 Phys/Coll after Motion10 Anim.
naming_conventions: Existing item symbol naming was preserved: itDosei_UnkMotionN_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotionN_Phys, itDosei_UnkMotionN_Coll, and it_3F14_Logic7_*.
New helpers follow local camel-case item style with the item prefix, such as itDosei_FacingAngle, itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAnimSpeed, and itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAttrs.
The matching-only constant-order helper is named sdata2_ordering, explicitly describing its section-ordering purpose rather than gameplay behavior.
Helper names distinguish dirty-matrix variants with the suffix WithMtxDirty, matching baselib HSD_JObj naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted volatile float and double constants in sdata2_ordering to control .sdata2 ordering.
Removed PAD_STACK calls after source reshaping produced the required stack layout naturally.
Used direct HSD_JObjSetRotationY and HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty calls with a separate angle helper to affect inlining/register allocation.
Replaced macro GET_ITEM(gobj) with gobj->user_data in selected functions to match codegen.
Added tiny accessor helpers for attributes and animation speed in itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim to influence expression ordering/inlining.
Included an explicit empty nested null check on jobj marked by an inline comment as 'permuterslop', evidence that source-shape permutation was used for matching.
Reordered Motion10 Phys/Coll definitions relative to Motion10 Anim.
Switched custom manual JObj rotation writes to WithMtxDirty baselib functions where dirty matrix behavior was part of the matched code shape.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt beyond the title records progress counts: Remaining item TUs: 18 and Remaining item functions: 63.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that use sdata2_ordering or volatile constants to fix .sdata2 layout.
Search for remaining PAD_STACK usages in src/melee/it/items and compare whether helper extraction can remove them.
Search for Dosei functions still named UnkMotion to identify future naming/behavior documentation work.
Compare GET_ITEM(gobj) versus gobj->user_data usage in matched item TUs for codegen-sensitive conventions.
Look for other JObj rotation code that manually writes rotate.x/y/z and can be replaced with HSD_JObjSetRotation*WithMtxDirty.
